english: perh. also ḱrá-mo (khra mo) piebald, two-coloured, (not many-coloured, which is bkrá-ba (bkra ba)); rgya-stág-ḱrá-bo (rgya stag khra bo) the streaked Indian tiger Mil.; *t́á-t́á* (*khra khra*) C. id.; *t́a-wo-pi-wo* (*khra wo pi wo*), t́a-si-pi-si* (*khra si pi si*) W. id. (spelling uncertain); nag-ḱrá (nag khra) black-spotted, so that black is the predominating colour of the whole; dmar-ḱrá (dmar khra) red-spotted, red being the predominating colour. - The significations of the various compounds of ḱra (khra) have all a reference to the peculiar effect produced on the eye by the blending of two or more colours together, especially when seen from a distance; so: ḱra-čam-mé (khra cam me) Glr. is said of a rainbow-tinted meteor, ḱra-lam-mé (khra lam me) Mil., (or lham-mé, (lham me,)) of a similar phenomenon, ḱra-čem-čém (khra chem chem) Pth. of a flight of birds; *t́a-čám-se* (*khra cam se*), t́a-čem-mé (*khra chem me*), t́a-čem-se* (*khra chem se*) C., *čam-t́a-t́in-nè* (*cam khra khrin ne*) Ld., *t́a-śig-ge* (*khra shig ge*) t́a-čig-ge (*khra cig ge*), t́a-róg-ge* (*khra rog ge*) C., - all these seem to be of nearly the same import. - These compounds have also assumed the character of an adverb, signifying, together, altogether, ḱra-me-ré (khra me re) Mil. id.
1. Cs. register, index. - 2. C. judicial decree. - 3. a species of grain, acc. to Wdn. = mgyogs-nás (mgyogs nas) a kind of barley growing and ripening within 60 days; v. nas (nas).

(in Bal. still pronounced *krag* (*khrag*), elsewhere *t́ag* (*khrag*)), Cs.: resp. sku-mtsal (sku mtshal), blood; *ńal-t́ág* (*mngal khrag*), *źań-t́ág* (*gzhang khrag*) W. vulg. blood discharged by menstruation, from which, acc. to some authorities, *pań-t́ág* (*pang khrag*) blood of the childbed is to be distinguished; rzuń-ḱrág (gzhung khrag) healthy, nourishing blood Cs.; nad-ḱrág (nad khrag) bad blood Cs.: ḱrag འdón-pa (khrag 'don pa), W. *t́ón-če* (*thon ce*), to bleed a person; gtár-ba (gtar ba) id.; ḱrag gčód-pa (khrag gcod pa) to stop, to stanch the blood; ḱrag čád-pa (khrag chad pa) vb. n. to cease to bleed, cessation of bleeding; *ńyiń-t́ág ḱól-la rag* (*snying khrag khol la rag*) W. I feel my blood boiling, e.g. from ascending a steep hill; ḱrag dzág-pa (khrag 'dzag pa) menstruation (the plain undisguised expression); ḱrag čág-pa (khrag chags pa) clotted blood, gore Cs.; ḱrag-śas-čé-ba (khrag shas che ba) plethoric Med. Comp. ḱrag-འḱrúgs (khrag 'khrugs) Sch.: agitation, flutter, orgasm of the blood. - ḱrág-čan (khrag can) bloody, e.g. ral-gri (ral gri). - ḱrag-gčód (khrag gcod) n. of a medicinal herb Med., ḱrag-čags-rtá (khrag chags rta) a 'blood-bred' horse, i.e. a real horse, opp. to a metaphysical one Mil. - ḱrag-འtúń (khrag 'thung) a class of terrifying deities Thgr. - *t́ág-tuń-bu* (*khrag 'thung bu*) W. leech. - ḱrag-gzér (khrag gzer) W. rheumatic pain (?) - ḱrag-ró (khrag ro) clotted blood (?) Med. ḱrag-líń (khrag ling) a clot of blood. - ḱrag-śór (khrag shor) hemorrhage, bloody flux (?) Med.
one hundred thousand million, an indefinitely large number Cs.; acc. to Lex. = प्रयुत one million, cf. dkrigs-pa (dkrigs pa).
Cs. complicate, confused; Zam.: like a troop of fighting men, or like the loose leaves of a book, when out of order.
Cs. stretched out; ḱrád-por sdód-pa (khrad por sdod pa) to sit (with the legs) stretched out (?). ḱrád-por skyé-ba (khrad por skye ba) Wdn. a botanical term applied to the leaves of plants.

shield, buckler; coat of mail, scales Sch.; acc. to oral communication the word in the first instance denotes scale (scale of a fish), and secondly coat of mail; consequently ḱráb-čan (khrab can) 1. scaled, scaly. 2. mailed, armed with a coat of mail; ḱrád-mḱan (khrad mkhan) armourer Glr.
1. a weeper, one that sheds tears on every occasion Sch. - 2. Mil. 92, 4?

a cut, a notch (in wood), lines cut into wood so as to cross one another, as an ornament; ḱrám-śiń (khram shing) a club-like implement, carved in the manner just mentioned, representing the attribute of a god. nyag-ḱrám (nyag khram) a notch.

I. C.: a liar, slu-bar byéd-pai ḱrám-pa (slu bar byed pa'i khram pa) Pth.; ḱrám-ma (khram ma) fem. Cs.; ḱa-ḱrám (kha khram) a lie Mil.; ḱrám-sems-čan (khram sems can) lying, mendacious Mil. - II W.: 1. lively, brisk, quick, like boys, kids etc. (the contrary of ylén-pa (glen pa) slow, indolent, apathetic); *t́ám-pa čò* (*khram pa co*) W. a wish of good speed, ad-
